fields.description:
Epic 1 of 2. Establishes the full kudos ledger infrastructure and 10 primary quality triggers for the process-reward tracking system. CONTEXT: Engineering teams adopting AI coding agents need a feedback system that reinforces quality behaviors at commit time. Without explicit recognition, agents optimize for task completion over quality practices. This epic delivers the ledger, anti-gaming stack, and 10 triggers so that every commit produces an auditable record of recognized behaviors. APPROACH: Implement ledger, award formula, anti-gaming controls, and 10 triggers as pure shell + Python stdlib anchored to commit-validate.sh in the existing commit workflow. Spikes S0/S1 de-risk sg pattern matching and Python AST clone detection before those triggers ship. SPRINT SCOPE RATIONALE: Infrastructure without triggers produces zero awards. All 10 triggers share one user goal (recognizing quality behaviors at commit time) and depend on the same anti-gaming stack. Spike tasks are investigation prerequisites, not sprint work items. SUCCESS CRITERIA: SC-E1-1: .kudos/ledger.jsonl (append-only JSONL) — fields: session_id, trigger_id, diff_hash, artifact_sha, verification_method, workflow_phase, message, points (int >= 1), jitter_factor (float in [0.8,1.2]), timestamp (ISO-8601 UTC); all string fields JSON-escaped; .kudos/ in .gitignore SC-E1-2: Award formula: Award(n) = round(BasePoints x (1/(n+1)) x jitter), jitter in [0.8,1.2] uniform random, min 1 point, floating-point division; n = prior session ledger entries for trigger_id SC-E1-3: Deduplication: one award per (trigger_id, diff_hash) per session SC-E1-4: Session ceiling: kudos.session_ceiling from dso-config.conf (default 100, validated >0; absent key uses default); awards blocked when reached, prohibition-block message shown SC-E1-5: kudos-write-guard.sh PreToolUse hook blocks Edit/Write to .kudos/** (exit 2); jq-free parse_json_field; registered via onboarding/architect-foundation SC-E1-6: commit-validate.sh integrated into COMMIT-WORKFLOW.md before commit gate; creates .kudos/ on first run; fails-open (any detection error skips with log, never aborts commit) SC-E1-7: Session identity UUID written to .kudos/session-id at worktree creation; commit-validate.sh reads from file (fails-open with fresh UUID if absent) SC-E1-8: Contributor rank displayed per commit: Contributor (0-25), Consistent (26-75), Distinguished (76-150), Exemplary (151-300), Principal Contributor (301+) SC-E1-9: CLAUDE.md Kudos section: overview, prohibition block (listed before rewards), jitter disclosure (+/-20% random), Goodhart's Law disclosure, rank table SC-E1-10: diff-to-sg-scope.sh wrapper: command -v sg pre-check (exit 2 if absent); checks HEAD~1 existence (exit 2 if first commit); git diff --name-only HEAD~1; sg run --pattern

--json=stream per file; parses NDJSON SC-E1-11: Spike S0 validates sg --json=stream exit code (0=no matches, 1=matches) and NDJSON schema before T5/T11 implementation SC-E1-12: Spike S1 implements/tests Python AST subtree hashing prototype (hashlib.sha256(ast.dump(node).encode())); measures false-positive rate; confirms jscpd file-output integration (cat $OUTPUT_DIR/jscpd-report.json, not stdout) if selected as fallback SC-E1-T3: reviewer-findings.json shows dimension score=5; one award per dimension per diff_hash SC-E1-T5: diff-to-sg-scope.sh detects function extracted to helper in staged Python files; grep fallback when sg absent SC-E1-T6: commit message references bug ticket ID; dso ticket show confirms type=bug AND created_at predates git merge-base HEAD main SC-E1-T7: staged Python file has >=4 control-flow nesting levels (if/for/while/with/try); depth via ast.NodeVisitor with counter (visit_If/For/While/With/Try increment, generic_visit decrement) SC-E1-T9: all staged Python files pass: git show ":$f" | ruff check --stdin-filename "$(pwd)/$f" - (staged blob, absolute path fixes ruff #17405 config-discovery bug) SC-E1-T11: diff-to-sg-scope.sh detects pass-through method removal; grep fallback when sg absent SC-E1-T12: staged diff removes commented-out code blocks SC-E1-T13: staged Python files show >=3 structurally identical AST subtrees removed (hashlib.sha256(ast.dump(node).encode()) fingerprinting; algorithm/threshold from Spike S1) SC-E1-T14: .review-events/ JSONL shows important/critical findings event followed by resubmission event in same session SC-E1-T15: .review-events/ JSONL shows blocked_by_test_gate:true commit followed by passing commit in same session; disqualified if only changes are .test-index RED marker lines (^\[.*\]$) SC-E1-23: Integration test seeds synthetic T9-qualifying commit, runs commit-validate.sh, asserts ledger contains T9 entry with points>0 AND stdout contains per-trigger breakdown RESEARCH FINDINGS: - External integration (sg/ast-grep): --json=stream NDJSON confirmed; command -v sg is correct absence-detection method (not exit code); duplicate block detection infeasible via sg (Python AST subtree hashing is the correct approach) - Novel pattern (PRM): 2025 research confirms step-level recognition reduces reasoning errors; immediate reward delivery critical for behavioral linkage; design aligns with AgentPRM and PRIME findings SCENARIO ANALYSIS: - diff-to-sg-scope.sh uses HEAD~1 diff during git commit --amend (medium, runtime): stale file set causes false T5/T7/T13 awards; implementation must detect amend context - T14 races with concurrent reviewer JSONL write (medium, runtime): partial JSONL line causes parse error; handle gracefully - kudos-write-guard.sh not registered after fresh clone (critical, deployment): hooks require onboarding re-run - .kudos/ absent from .gitignore (high, deployment): commit-validate.sh must add .kudos/ to .gitignore on first run - Missing kudos.session_ceiling key causes crash (high, configuration): validate/default at startup - sg absent in CI silently voids sg-dependent triggers (medium, deployment): document or accept skip as designed COORDINATION: Soft coordination with 1083-fb3d (local workflow simplification) for hook registration ordering.
